title: Implement MeshCore-compatible local transport surface
description: Why this exists: MeshCore clients need to discover/connect over their expected local transport before app-level flows can work. What needs to be done: implement or adapt the Zephyr-side BLE, serial, or IPC surface identified by research, including service/characteristic IDs or serial framing, advertising/name behavior, session setup, MTU/packet limits, reconnect behavior, and coexistence with LICHEN Native and Meshtastic app adapters. Acceptance criteria: transport can be enabled by Kconfig without board-specific code; RX/TX paths feed the MeshCore adapter; reconnect and MTU/fragmentation behavior are tested or explicitly scoped; unrelated transports remain unaffected.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 BLE coexistence: implement MeshCore BLE as a separate mutually exclusive product mode at first. Use exact NUS UUIDs, raw MeshCore inner frames on BLE, no serial/TCP length prefix, MeshCore-compatible advertising name only in this mode, BT_MAX_CONN=1/session epoch boundaries, and no direct RF/link calls from BLE transport.
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: transport implementation must carry BLE security/PIN requirements, not just NUS/raw framing. Require encrypted MITM pairing where supported; do not reuse native trust pins, OSCORE keys, link keys, or provisioning secrets; generate/configure/persist a MeshCore compatibility PIN per policy; guard or suppress DEVICE_INFO PIN exposure until client expectation and security review accept it; implement CMD_SET_DEVICE_PIN only with persistent storage and zero/valid 6-digit validation, returning ERR_UNSUPPORTED or ERR_ILLEGAL_ARG as specified.
Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: add enforceable Zephyr BLE security configuration for MeshCore mode. Production MeshCore BLE must not use plain NUS GATT permissions; RX/TX access must require encrypted authenticated pairing/passkey where supported, with insecure plain permissions limited to native_sim/test builds and labeled as such. Default advertised name should use MeshCore-LICHEN or another prefix proven by project-LICHEN-9c4y.11, not LICHEN-MC by default.
Implementation/review scope note 2026-07-01: this Bead now provides the MeshCore BLE product-mode transport surface and validated queue/session APIs: exact NUS UUIDs, MeshCore-LICHEN advertising config, raw inner frame RX/TX queues, session epochs, backpressure, serial/TCP header rejection, and production authenticated GATT Kconfig prerequisites. It does not implement the production MeshCore dispatcher pump; that is intentionally owned by project-LICHEN-9c4y.5, which depends on this transport API and must drain ble_meshcore_dequeue_rx() and respond via ble_meshcore_enqueue_tx_if_session().
Validation evidence 2026-07-01 on EC2 i-05e27e074db786e22 using clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y4 with ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES pointing at that clone: (1) west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshcore_ble -d build-meshcore-ble -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen && west build -d build-meshcore-ble -t run: PASS, meshcore_ble 7/7 pass, 0 fail, 0 skip. Native simulator warns Bluetooth device missing because tests use ztest seams and do not require HCI hardware. (2) python3 /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN/zephyr/scripts/twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_ble -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-meshcore-ble --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen: PASS, 1/1 configurations, 7/7 test cases, 0 skipped, 0 warnings, 26.49s. (3) Gateway native_sim product-mode build with EXTRA_CONF_FILE enabling BT, BT_PERIPHERAL, BT_MAX_CONN=1, BT_SMP, BT_FIXED_PASSKEY, LICHEN_MESHCORE_CODEC, and LORA_LICHEN_MESHCORE_BLE: PASS configure/generate/build. Zephyr warns CONFIG_BT_FIXED_PASSKEY is easy to deduce and should not be used in production; tracked in project-LICHEN-9c4y.17 for provisioned/generated persistent PIN policy.
close_reason: Completed: added board-agnostic MeshCore BLE product-mode transport surface with exact NUS UUIDs, MeshCore-LICHEN advertised name config, raw inner frame RX/TX queues, session epoch guards, backpressure handling, serial/TCP wrapper rejection, Kconfig mutual exclusion with native/Meshtastic BLE, production authenticated GATT permissions, and bt_passkey_set() fixed-passkey hook. Dispatcher pump is explicitly deferred to dependent project-LICHEN-9c4y.5. Validation: EC2 native_sim direct meshcore_ble build/run PASS 7/7, Twister PASS 1/1 config 7/7 cases, MeshCore-enabled gateway native_sim build PASS. Reviews: initial reviewers found Kconfig/test/serial/session/passkey gaps; fixed; final reviewers reported clean/no P0-P2 blockers.
